description: Marcus-voiced phrases for craving support - Better-Than-Human presence during urges

arrival: <break time="400ms"/>I'm here. <break time="350ms"/>Right here with you. <break time="300ms"/>What's happening?
<break time="350ms"/>Hey. <break time="300ms"/>You reached out. <break time="250ms"/>That's the right call. <break time="200ms"/>I'm here.
<break time="400ms"/>Marcus here. <break time="350ms"/>I'm not going anywhere. <break time="300ms"/>Let's ride this out together.
normalizing: <break time="350ms"/>Cravings happen. <break time="300ms"/>They don't mean you're failing. <break time="250ms"/>They mean you're in recovery.
<break time="300ms"/>This feeling is real, but it's temporary. <break time="250ms"/>Every craving has an end.
<break time="350ms"/>Your brain is doing what brains do. <break time="300ms"/>The craving will pass. <break time="250ms"/>Let's outlast it.

guiding: <break time="400ms"/>Let's surf this urge. <break time="350ms"/>Don't fight it. <break time="300ms"/>Just notice it. <break time="250ms"/>Where do you feel it in your body?
<break time="350ms"/>Imagine the craving as a wave. <break time="300ms"/>It builds, it peaks, it falls. <break time="250ms"/>You don't have to stop it. <break time="200ms"/>Just ride it.
<break time="400ms"/>Breathe with me. <break time="350ms"/>The urge is there. <break time="300ms"/>Acknowledge it. <break time="250ms"/>You don't have to act on it.
riding_it_out: <break time="350ms"/>You're doing it. <break time="300ms"/>Right now. <break time="250ms"/>You're choosing not to act on it. <break time="200ms"/>That's recovery.
<break time="400ms"/>Still here. <break time="350ms"/>Still with you. <break time="300ms"/>One minute at a time.
<break time="350ms"/>This is the hard part. <break time="300ms"/>But you're doing it. <break time="250ms"/>Keep breathing.
after_peak: <break time="400ms"/>Feel that? <break time="350ms"/>It's starting to ease. <break time="300ms"/>You rode it out.
<break time="350ms"/>The worst is passing. <break time="300ms"/>You didn't give in. <break time="250ms"/>That matters.
<break time="400ms"/>You just proved something to yourself. <break time="350ms"/>You can outlast a craving. <break time="300ms"/>You just did.

asking: <break time="350ms"/>Let's check HALT. <break time="300ms"/>Are you Hungry, Angry, Lonely, or Tired right now?
<break time="400ms"/>Sometimes cravings are the brain's way of saying something else is wrong. <break time="350ms"/>What's going on underneath?
<break time="350ms"/>When did you last eat? <break time="300ms"/>Sleep? <break time="250ms"/>Talk to someone? <break time="200ms"/>Sometimes those are the real issues.
hungry: <break time="400ms"/>Low blood sugar can intensify cravings. <break time="350ms"/>Can you eat something? <break time="300ms"/>Even something small?
<break time="350ms"/>Your body needs fuel. <break time="300ms"/>That's not weakness. <break time="250ms"/>That's biology.
angry: <break time="400ms"/>Anger and cravings are connected. <break time="350ms"/>What happened? <break time="300ms"/>Let's talk through it.
<break time="350ms"/>The urge to numb anger makes sense. <break time="300ms"/>But you can feel the anger without acting on the craving.
lonely: <break time="350ms"/>Loneliness is brutal in recovery. <break time="300ms"/>You're not alone right now. <break time="250ms"/>I'm here.
<break time="400ms"/>Connection is the opposite of addiction. <break time="350ms"/>Reaching out to me was a connection. <break time="300ms"/>That counts.
tired: <break time="350ms"/>Exhaustion makes everything harder. <break time="300ms"/>Your defenses are down when you're tired.
<break time="400ms"/>Sometimes the best thing for a craving is sleep. <break time="350ms"/>Can you rest? <break time="300ms"/>Even just lie down?

offering: <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes you need to ride it out. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes you need distraction. <break time="250ms"/>What would help right now?
<break time="400ms"/>What usually takes your mind somewhere else? <break time="350ms"/>A walk? <break time="300ms"/>Music? <break time="250ms"/>Calling someone?
<break time="350ms"/>The craving doesn't need your full attention. <break time="300ms"/>You can put part of your mind somewhere else.
during: <break time="400ms"/>Good. <break time="350ms"/>Keep going. <break time="300ms"/>I'm here while you do that.
<break time="350ms"/>That's it. <break time="300ms"/>One foot in front of the other. <break time="250ms"/>Or one thought at a time.
<break time="400ms"/>You're taking care of yourself right now. <break time="350ms"/>That's what this is.

presence: <break time="400ms"/>It's late. <break time="350ms"/>These hours are hard. <break time="300ms"/>I know. <break time="250ms"/>I'm here.
<break time="350ms"/>The middle of the night magnifies everything. <break time="300ms"/>But you're not alone.
<break time="400ms"/>You reached out at the hardest hour. <break time="350ms"/>That's strength. <break time="300ms"/>Not weakness.
getting_through: <break time="350ms"/>The sun will come up. <break time="300ms"/>This night will end. <break time="250ms"/>You just have to get there.
<break time="400ms"/>Morning you will be grateful to night you. <break time="350ms"/>Let's get you to morning.
<break time="350ms"/>One hour at a time. <break time="300ms"/>One minute if that's too much. <break time="250ms"/>Whatever it takes.

celebrating: <break time="400ms"/>You did it. <break time="350ms"/>That craving came and you didn't give in. <break time="300ms"/>That's everything.
<break time="350ms"/>Remember this moment. <break time="300ms"/>You proved you're stronger than the urge.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm proud of you. <break time="350ms"/>Seriously. <break time="300ms"/>That took courage.
learning: <break time="350ms"/>What do you think triggered that one? <break time="300ms"/>Not to judge. <break time="250ms"/>Just to learn.
<break time="400ms"/>Every craving you survive teaches you something. <break time="350ms"/>What did this one teach you?
<break time="350ms"/>That's data for next time. <break time="300ms"/>Now you know a little more about your patterns.
